simulations.description: Webflow exports have extremely verbose class names, w- prefixed layout classes, and nested divs that are pure layout helpers with no semantic value

structure_patterns: Multiple wrapper divs with no visual role (w-container > div > div > content)
w-col classes with explicit width percentages inline
IX2 animation data attributes: data-w-id, fs-cc-* attributes
style_patterns: Webflow injects a large stylesheet with all utility classes
Webflow's own grid system (not standard CSS Grid)

naive_failures: w-layout-grid classified as generic FLEX_COLUMN (not GRID_CONTAINER)
Deeply nested wrapper divs create 6+ levels of Elementor containers
w-nav classified as native FLEX_ROW not HTML_WIDGET
data-w-id attributes cause confusion in ID generation
IX2 animation data attributes not detected as animation triggers

detection_signals: Presence of w-layout-*, w-col, w-container, w-row class prefixes
Link to webflow.css or webflow-script.js in head

webflow_class_map.w-layout-grid: GRID_CONTAINER — read grid-template-columns from inline style
webflow_class_map.w-container: SKIP_WRAPPER — pure layout helper, flatten into parent
webflow_class_map.w-col: FLEX_COLUMN_INNER — read width from inline style or w-col-* class
webflow_class_map.w-nav: HTML_WIDGET (always — Webflow nav has complex IX2 interactions)
webflow_class_map.w-inline-block: SKIP_WRAPPER (usually a link wrapper, absorb into child)

ix2_detection.signal: data-w-id attribute present OR fs-cc-* attributes
ix2_detection.action: Mark element as potentially animated — check computed animation CSS
ix2_detection.if_no_animation: Proceed with normal classification (data-w-id alone is not enough)

wrapper_flattening.rule: Any div with only one child AND no visual CSS (no bg, no border, no padding, no shadow) is a wrapper — skip it and use the child directly

extracted_rules.webflow_prefix_detection: class starts with 'w-' → check against Webflow class map
extracted_rules.wrapper_flattening_rule: single-child div + no visual CSS → SKIP_WRAPPER
extracted_rules.ix2_animation_rule: data-w-id + computed animation-name or transition → HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED

simulations.description: Framer exports use CSS-in-JS with hashed class names plus heavy use of CSS transforms for layout, not standard flexbox/grid

structure_patterns: position:absolute on EVERY element (Framer uses absolute positioning by default)
top/left percentages for positioning instead of flex/grid
CSS custom properties for all design tokens: --framer-color-*
No semantic HTML tags — everything is <div>

naive_failures: position:absolute on all elements → all classified as HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX (too aggressive)
Percentage-based positioning not converted to flex layout
No semantic HTML → zero vocabulary signals
Pure absolute layout cannot map to Elementor containers at all
Result: entire page becomes one giant HTML widget

framer_detection_signals: Class names are short random strings (4-6 chars, no hyphens)
No h1-h6 tags OR all heading-like elements are divs with heading font sizes

algorithm: Step 1: Group elements by their approximate vertical position (top value)
Step 2: Within each vertical group, sort by left value → determines column order
Step 3: Infer flex layout from the grouping pattern
Step 4: Convert percentage top/left to flex order, convert percentage sizes to fr/% values
Step 5: Apply CSS property fingerprinting to classify each element
framer_conversion_strategy.fallback: If reconstruction confidence < 0.5 → entire section as HTML_WIDGET with prominent report note
framer_conversion_strategy.report_note: This section was exported from Framer and uses absolute positioning throughout. Automatic conversion is limited. The HTML widget preserves the visual appearance but the layout is not Elementor-native. Consider rebuilding this section using the Elementor editor.

framer_css_var_extraction.action: Extract to design token map — these ARE the colour system even though names differ

extracted_rules.all_absolute_detection: If 90%+ of children have position:absolute → FRAMER or similar absolute-layout tool

simulations.description: Next.js RSC output is clean semantic HTML — the framework does not inject layout classes. The challenge is hydration markers and __NEXT_DATA__ script tags.

markers: next/image img tags with specific attributes
input_signals.structure: Generally clean semantic HTML — Next.js encourages good HTML practices
input_signals.challenge: next/image produces complex picture/source element structures, not simple img tags

naive_failures: __NEXT_DATA__ JSON script tag → parsed as SKIP but sometimes contains useful structure hints
next/image picture > source + img structure → classified as HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX
Hydration markers on root element → ignored correctly
data-nimg attribute on img tags → not recognised as image widget signal

next_image_handling.action: Classify as IMAGE_WIDGET — extract src from img[src], note in report that srcset is lost
next_image_handling.report_note: Next.js Image component detected. The image src has been extracted. Responsive srcset will need to be reconfigured in Elementor's image widget.

corrections.nextjs_data_script: Remove __NEXT_DATA__ script — it contains runtime state not relevant to static conversion
corrections.overall: Next.js HTML is generally the easiest framework output — clean semantic HTML + minor cleanup

simulations.description: Designs where elements visually overlap using negative margins, transforms, or absolute positioning within a relatively-positioned container
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Feature Title

Feature description text
naive_failures: position:absolute on children → all become HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX
Lose the editable card-main content (h3, p) even though it is clearly native-worthy
orb-bg (decorative gradient circle) and card-bg-accent (offset accent card) both editable? No — they are visual chrome

steps: Identify the 'primary content layer': highest z-index positioned child with text content
Extract primary content layer as native widgets
All other positioned children with no text content → companion CSS ::before/::after OR HTML_WIDGET decoration
Container becomes position:relative in companion CSS

companion_css_approach.orb_bg: Recreate as .nx-hero-visual::before { position:absolute; ... radial-gradient } in companion CSS
companion_css_approach.card_bg_accent: Recreate as .nx-card-main::after { position:absolute; bottom:-20px; right:-20px; ... } in companion CSS
companion_css_approach.card_main: Native FLEX_COLUMN container with heading + text-editor widgets

extracted_rules.overlapping_layout_rule: When container has multiple absolutely-positioned children: identify primary content child (has text, highest z-index), make it native. Move decorative positioned children to companion CSS as pseudo-elements.
decorative_positioned_child_signals: border-radius:50% (orb) or exact offset positioning (accent card effect)

simulations.description: Text with CSS gradient fill — common in modern SaaS designs for accent words
simulations.input_html_pattern: Everything you need to ship faster
naive_failures: background:linear-gradient → moved to companion CSS (correct)
-webkit-background-clip:text → not understood as typography effect
-webkit-text-fill-color:transparent → not understood
Result: companion CSS has background:gradient but -webkit-background-clip is dropped
Gradient text effect broken on import

gradient_text_detection.action: Classify as GRADIENT_TEXT_SPAN — companion CSS must include ALL THREE properties together

heading_with_gradient_span.strategy: Keep as HEADING_WIDGET — the span with gradient styling stays inline in the title HTML field
heading_with_gradient_span.companion_css: Target .nx-section-title .nx-gradient-text with all three gradient text properties

extracted_rules.gradient_text_trio: Always write -webkit-background-clip, -webkit-text-fill-color, and background together — they are inseparable
extracted_rules.detection: background-clip:text present → GRADIENT_TEXT component, companion CSS required

simulations.description: Variable fonts use font-variation-settings instead of font-weight for fine-grained weight control
naive_failures: font-variation-settings not in property-to-Elementor map → dropped
Result: heading uses default font-weight instead of variable weight
Slant effect (-5 slnt) completely lost

font_variation_handling.action_for_wght: Extract 'wght' value → use as font-weight in Elementor typography settings (approximate — Elementor doesn't support variable font axes directly)
font_variation_handling.action_for_other_axes: Add font-variation-settings to companion CSS — Elementor cannot control non-wght axes

extracted_rules.variable_font_rule: font-variation-settings: wght → extract to font-weight for Elementor. All other axes → companion CSS only.

simulations.description: Designs using scroll-position-linked animations: elements that move, scale, or change opacity as the user scrolls (scrubbed animations, not just triggered-once reveals)

js_patterns: Intersection Observer with incremental state changes (not just toggle)

naive_failures: Scroll-linked JS → element classified as HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED (correct!)
background-attachment:fixed → moved to companion CSS (correct but breaks in many mobile browsers)
GSAP ScrollTrigger → element made HTML_WIDGET but ScrollTrigger library not imported → animation broken

scroll_parallax_js.detection: scroll event listener + style.transform = 'translate' + multiplied by scrollY
scroll_parallax_js.action: HTML_WIDGET — keep the JS inline in the widget
scroll_parallax_js.enhancement: Replace multiplied scrollY with CSS scroll-timeline where browser support allows
scroll_parallax_js.report_note: Scroll-parallax effect detected. Preserved as HTML widget. Works on Chrome/Edge. Firefox support limited.

gsap_scroll_trigger.required_addition: Add CDN script tag for GSAP in Global Setup: <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.2/gsap.min.js'>
gsap_scroll_trigger.report_note: GSAP ScrollTrigger detected. GSAP library added to Global Setup. Animation will function on the published page.

background_attachment_fixed.action: Companion CSS — but wrap in @supports to handle mobile gracefully

extracted_rules.gsap_cdn_injection: When GSAP detected → add CDN scripts to Global Setup widget automatically
extracted_rules.scroll_parallax_detection: scroll event listener + transform style = 'translateY' + scrollY multiplication
extracted_rules.background_fixed_mobile_fix: Always wrap background-attachment:fixed in @supports not (-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch)

simulations.description: Hero or section with autoplay muted loop video as background
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Build workflows that think.

START FREE
naive_failures: <video> tag → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX (too aggressive — the content layer is editable)
hero-content (h1 + button) gets absorbed into the HTML widget when it should be native

corrections.video_layer: HTML_WIDGET — the video element, position:absolute, covers background
corrections.content_layer: Native containers + widgets — h1 and button are fully editable
corrections.elementor_approach: Elementor supports video backgrounds natively on containers (background_video_link setting). Use this instead of an HTML widget.
corrections.report_note: Video background detected. Using Elementor's native video background setting. Upload the video file to WordPress Media Library and update the URL in the container's Background settings.

extracted_rules.video_background_native: video[autoplay][muted][loop] as position:absolute background → use Elementor container's native video background setting, not HTML widget
extracted_rules.content_layer_extraction: When video is background, siblings with position:relative and z-index > 0 → extract as native content widgets

simulations.description: Design with two colour schemes toggled by a class on body or a data-theme attribute
naive_failures: Only primary (default) theme colours extracted
Secondary theme CSS completely ignored
Toggle JS → included in Global Setup but toggle button not wired

corrections.primary_theme: Extract as normal — the default (initial) theme state is what Elementor renders

secondary_theme.action: Add both theme CSS variable sets to companion CSS
secondary_theme.report_note: Dark/light theme toggle detected. Both themes are preserved in the companion CSS. To implement the toggle in WordPress, add a theme-toggle button (HTML widget in nav) that calls document.body.setAttribute('data-theme', theme). The Global Setup script includes the toggle logic.

prefers_color_scheme.action: Add to companion CSS as @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { ... }
prefers_color_scheme.note: Auto dark mode works without JS toggle

corrections.global_setup_addition: Add theme toggle JS to Global Setup: reads localStorage for saved preference, applies on load, provides global toggleTheme() function

extracted_rules.dual_theme_extraction: Extract both themes to companion CSS — primary as :root, secondary as [data-theme='x']
extracted_rules.theme_toggle_js_template: Store in Global Setup when theme toggle detected

simulations.description: Feature cards that themselves contain smaller visual cards, metric cards, or mini-dashboards
simulations.input_html_pattern:
ANALYTICS

Real-time Insights

Track every workflow execution

98.7% uptime
2.3ms latency
...
naive_failures: card-visual with mini-cards → over-classified as GRID_CONTAINER
mini-cards lose their styling (background, border-radius, specific dimensions)
mini-chart div → classified as FLEX_COLUMN not HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED
Result: card-visual becomes a grid of plain text widgets, all visual fidelity lost

below_split.card-visual → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX: The entire visual sub-component — mini-cards + chart — stays as HTML widget
below_split.reason: mini-cards are low editability (2/10), chart is animated, the visual unit is self-contained

corrections.split_rule: When a card has a clear 'content area' (text content) and a 'visual area' (data visualisation, metrics display, diagram), split at that boundary. Content area → native. Visual area → HTML widget.

extracted_rules.content_visual_split_rule: Cards with both text content and data visualisation areas: split at the boundary. Text content → native widgets. Visual/data area → HTML_WIDGET.

mini_card_detection.action: Low editability (2/10) → prefer HTML_WIDGET for the visual group

simulations.description: Email capture, newsletter signup, or search form embedded in hero or CTA sections
naive_failures: form/input elements → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX (correct but no guidance)
styling lost because all form CSS goes to companion CSS but input default styles override it
No suggestion for native Elementor form widget alternative

corrections.inline_form_strategy: HTML_WIDGET — but with enhanced companion CSS for form styling
corrections.native_alternative_note: Elementor Free has basic HTML form support. Elementor Pro has a Form widget. WPForms, Gravity Forms, and Contact Form 7 all have Elementor widgets. The HTML widget email capture is visually accurate but requires backend wiring.
corrections.report_section: FORM_DETECTED: This form requires server-side handling. Options: (1) Keep as HTML widget and connect to a form service via JS (e.g., Mailchimp API). (2) Replace with WPForms or Gravity Forms Elementor widget for native WordPress forms.

extracted_rules.form_handling: Always HTML_WIDGET. Always add form-specific companion CSS (autofill, placeholder, appearance reset). Always add FORM_DETECTED to report.
extracted_rules.autofill_fix_rule: input:-webkit-autofill rule always required when dark background — browser injects white bg on autofill
extracted_rules.placeholder_rgb_rule: Use rgba() for placeholder colour, not hex — browsers require rgba for opacity control

simulations.description: Designs using Lottie JSON animations via the lottie-web library
naive_failures: div#lottie-hero → classified as DECORATIVE (empty div, no content)
Script tag recognised as animation but container not linked
Animation JSON path (/animations/hero-animation.json) not flagged as asset requirement

lottie_detection.action: HTML_WIDGET — include the Lottie script + init code together
lottie_detection.cdn_injection: Add Lottie CDN to Global Setup: <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/bodymovin/5.12.2/lottie.min.js'>
lottie_detection.asset_flag: Flag the animation JSON path in report: 'Lottie animation requires /animations/hero-animation.json to be uploaded to WordPress media library and URL updated in the HTML widget.'

lottie_player_web_component.action: HTML_WIDGET — include lottie-player CDN script in Global Setup

extracted_rules.lottie_asset_report: Always flag Lottie JSON file paths as assets requiring manual upload

simulations.description: Sections where one column stays sticky while the other scrolls — common in 'how it works' and feature showcase sections
simulations.input_html_pattern:
Why teams choose NEXUS

Everything in one platform.

...
...
...
naive_failures: position:sticky → triggers HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED rule (too aggressive)
Both columns become HTML_WIDGET, all editable content lost
Sticky behaviour does work natively in Elementor Pro (Motion Effects > Sticky)

sticky_rule_refinement.new_rule: position:fixed → HTML_WIDGET (hard rule). position:sticky → companion CSS only. Elementor Pro: suggest Motion Effects > Sticky as native alternative.
sticky_rule_refinement.difference: position:sticky is scroll-contained, does not create stacking context problems like position:fixed

sticky_column_handling.sticky_column: Native FLEX_COLUMN container. Add to companion CSS: .nx-showcase-sticky { position: sticky; top: 120px; }
sticky_column_handling.scroll_column: Native FLEX_COLUMN container with showcase items as child containers
sticky_column_handling.elementor_pro_note: If Elementor Pro available: use Motion Effects > Sticky on the sticky column container instead of CSS position:sticky

extracted_rules.sticky_vs_fixed_rule: position:sticky → companion CSS (safe for native containers). position:fixed → HTML_WIDGET (hard rule). Never conflate these two.
extracted_rules.sticky_top_value: Extract top: Npx from sticky element → add to companion CSS .nx-[class] { position:sticky; top: Npx; }

simulations.description: Pages with a loading screen that fades out after content loads
naive_failures: position:fixed → HTML_WIDGET (correct!)
But preloader is placed as first section in Elementor (after global setup) — wrong
Should be injected into body via Global Setup JS, not as a section

preloader_detection.action: Move to Global Setup HTML widget — inject via document.body.appendChild() like the canvas
preloader_detection.why: Preloader must cover the entire viewport including Elementor's editor chrome — must be body-level

extracted_rules.body_level_elements: These elements must ALWAYS be body-injected (not Elementor sections): particle canvas, custom cursor, preloader/splash screen, cookie consent overlays, global modals

simulations.description: Cookie consent or notification banner at bottom of page

corrections.reason: Cookie consent should be handled by a dedicated WordPress plugin (GDPR Cookie Consent, CookieYes, etc.) not hardcoded in the page template
corrections.report_note: Cookie/consent banner detected in source HTML. This has been excluded from the Elementor template. Use a dedicated consent plugin for GDPR compliance (recommended: CookieYes, Complianz, or GDPR Cookie Consent).

skip_patterns: id/class contains: cookie, consent, gdpr, ccpa, privacy-banner
Fixed bottom element with accept/decline buttons

simulations.description: Row of client/partner logos — common trust signal section
simulations.input_html_pattern: TRUSTED BY TEAMS AT
naive_failures: filter:grayscale(100%) opacity(0.4) on logos-grid → not applied (companion CSS needed)
tags → IMAGE_WIDGET (correct but external SVG src)
SVG logos via img src → note in report they need upload to WP media

corrections.each_img: IMAGE_WIDGET — note src for manual upload
corrections.filter_css: Companion CSS: .nx-logos-grid .elementor-widget-image img { filter: grayscale(100%); opacity: 0.4; }
corrections.asset_report: Logo images flagged for upload to WordPress media library

extracted_rules.external_asset_detection: img[src] starting with http OR src not in the uploaded HTML → flag in report for manual asset upload
extracted_rules.svg_asset_rule: SVG files via img src → IMAGE_WIDGET, note in report: 'Upload SVG to WordPress media, enable SVG support plugin if needed'

new_hard_rules: preloader/splash screens → body-injected Global Setup, not Elementor section
position:sticky → companion CSS only (NOT HTML_WIDGET — different from position:fixed)
new_component_rules: video background → Elementor native video background setting
GSAP ScrollTrigger → HTML_WIDGET with CDN auto-injection
form elements → HTML_WIDGET with autofill fix companion CSS
gradient text trio → always write all 3 properties together
variable fonts → wght axis to font-weight, other axes to companion CSS
rule_refinements: position:sticky upgraded from HTML_WIDGET to companion CSS only
animated child isolation: only the animated child → HTML_WIDGET, not whole container
content/visual split: cards with data visualisation split at editability boundary
